vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1696: feat: decouple merge-train from the Flywheel — per-project pipeline feature + multi-project view

plan.title: feat: decouple merge-train from the Flywheel — per-project pipeline feature + multi-project view
plan.author: agent:claude-fable-5

references.label: PRD draft (glossary, FR/NFR, decisions D1-D10, verified file:line references)

narratives.Problem: The merge-train (per-issue auto-merge policy, conflict-aware merge order, post-merge sibling reconciler, UAT batch trains) is a per-project pipeline concern but is wired as a Flywheel feature: (1) the enable flag is flywheel.merge_train_enabled (src/lib/database/app-settings.ts:86); (2) the UAT-train ready set is computed from the live flywheel run's activePipeline and process.cwd() — getReadySet() in src/dashboard/server/services/uat-train.ts:63 returns null with no run, and projectRoot() (:58) pins everything to the Panopticon repo. This is exactly why a ready MIN-831 never got a MYN UAT generation while the run was pan-scoped (2026-06-11 operator report); (3) the UI lives only on the Flywheel page. Additionally: the active run's effective scope is not persisted or displayed (scope is baked into the spawn prompt at flywheel-actions.ts:223,294), the Settings scope select (RolesPanel.tsx:554-566) lacks explanatory copy and carries an inaccurate 'applies on the next tick' line, and roles/flywheel.md:83 hardcodes 'list PAN issues' without ever defining all-tracked-projects.
narratives.Proposal: Make the merge-train a first-class per-project pipeline feature. (1) Move the flag to merge_train.enabled with a legacy fallback read (the operator's current ON state must survive deploy), plus a tri-state per-project override in projects.yaml (enabled/disabled/absent=follow global) resolved by isMergeTrainEnabledForProject() and honored by both the UAT train reconciler and the post-merge sibling reconciler. (2) Source the ready set per project from review-status records (getAllReviewStatusesFromDb + mergeGateEligibility as the authoritative gate, deaconIgnored excluded), grouped via resolveProjectFromIssueSync — no flywheel run required; titles from the server issue cache with issueId fallback. (3) The UAT train reconciler iterates all tracked projects per tick, performing zero git operations for projects with no ready work and no live generations. (4) New aggregate API under /api/merge-train/* (queues, generations, stack, promote, assemble, merge-next); legacy flywheel-namespaced routes are removed in a final cleanup bead after the frontend migrates, so every intermediate bead leaves a working tree. (5) Frontend: the MergeQueueCard becomes a multi-project merge-train view with a persisted project filter, hosted on the Awaiting Merge page; the Flywheel page stays one viewer; the project cockpit gets the per-project override control plus a compact queue/generation summary. (6) Scope UX: persist effective scope on the run record at start/resume and display it (with configured-vs-run mismatch visible), fix the settings select copy/help text, and define scope semantics in roles/flywheel.md. (7) Docs sweep disambiguating orchestrator scope from per-project batch-train independence. Everything below the service layer (per-root reconciler, project_root column, per-issue project resolution in merge-train-deps, gen.projectRoot stacks) is already multi-project-capable — the work concentrates in wiring, routes, flag, and UI. Full design with before/after snippets and decisions D1-D10: .pan/drafts/PAN-1696.md.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Cross-project UAT batches: generations remain strictly per-project; only the view/control surface spans projects
- A new `pan merge-train` CLI verb or wrapper skill; CLI exposure stays within `pan flywheel status`
- Renaming flywheel.require_uat_before_merge or changing the shouldHoldForUat 3-tier auto-merge policy
- Code-enforced multi-project flywheel inventory; scope semantics remain prompt-level instruction to the orchestrator LLM
- PAN-1691 §5d live-cascade validation and closing cascade bugs PAN-1240/1215/1213/1658
- Backward-compat aliases for the removed /api/flywheel merge-train endpoints (dashboard frontend is the only consumer)
- Module/file renames (src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts keeps its path)
- Hardening non-PAN UAT stacks beyond per-project domain resolution

autoDecisions.summary: Flag migration is a fallback read (read merge_train.enabled, fall back to flywheel.merge_train_enabled when unset; writes target only the new key) — no data migration.
autoDecisions.rationale: Zero-risk upgrade; the flag is ON in production and must not silently flip off.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-project override is tri-state (enabled/disabled/absent = follow global), mirroring auto_merge_default, and gates BOTH the UAT train reconciler and the post-merge sibling reconciler.
autoDecisions.rationale: Preserves current behavior on upgrade while giving an opt-out valve per project now that the global flag reaches every tracked repo's git.

autoDecisions.summary: Ready-set sourcing uses mergeGateEligibility directly (plus deaconIgnored exclusion) over getAllReviewStatusesFromDb, dropping the MERGE_GATE_VERBS verb filter; the verb wrapper and MERGE_GATE_VERBS are deleted in the cleanup bead if grep shows no remaining non-test callers.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1759 made eligibility authoritative; the verb was only the orchestrator's intent signal, needed solely because activePipeline was the data source. The readyForMerge boolean can be stale — the predicate is the truth.

autoDecisions.summary: New routes live under an aggregate /api/merge-train/* namespace (queues/generations return all projects; mutations take project or generation-name params); old /api/flywheel merge-train routes are removed in a final cleanup bead after the frontend migrates, with no aliases.
autoDecisions.rationale: The dashboard frontend is the only consumer; deferring removal keeps every bead independently mergeable with a working tree.

autoDecisions.summary: The multi-project view is hosted on the Awaiting Merge page; the Flywheel page stays a viewer; no new top-level page. The project cockpit gets the per-project control plus a compact summary (ready count + current generation name/status) linking to Awaiting Merge.
autoDecisions.rationale: Awaiting Merge is already the cross-project ready-work surface the issue names; cockpit owns per-project settings (PAN-1693 pattern).

autoDecisions.summary: UAT stack frontend host resolves from the project's dns domain config with pan.localhost fallback; non-PAN stack support beyond URL resolution is best-effort.
autoDecisions.rationale: Minimal change that unblocks MYN; ensureDevcontainerSync already renders per-project templates.

autoDecisions.summary: Effective scope is persisted on the run record at start/resume; UI copy states scope changes apply at the next run start or resume, and a configured-vs-run mismatch is made visible.
autoDecisions.rationale: Scope is baked into the spawn prompt (flywheel-actions.ts:223,294); the running orchestrator's prompt cannot retro-change — honest semantics.

autoDecisions.summary: No interactive questions asked (auto mode); decisions D1-D10 in .pan/drafts/PAN-1696.md record all judgment calls with rationale and verified code references.

items.title: Move flag to merge_train.enabled with legacy fallback read

narrative.Action: In src/lib/database/app-settings.ts:77-150: add MERGE_TRAIN_ENABLED_KEY = 'merge_train.enabled'; rename the old constant to LEGACY_FLYWHEEL_MERGE_TRAIN_ENABLED_KEY. isMergeTrainEnabled(): read the new key; when null, read the legacy key; default false. setMergeTrainEnabled(): write the new key only. The flywheel config GET/POST payload (src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts:221-250) keeps exposing merge_train_enabled, now backed by the renamed helpers. Add unit tests covering new-key read, legacy fallback (ON value stored only under the old key), and write target. Before/after snippet in PRD work item 1.

subItems.title: isMergeTrainEnabled() returns the value stored under merge_train.enabled when that key exists, and falls back to flywheel.merge_train_enabled when the new key is absent (unit test covers an ON value stored only under the old key)
setMergeTrainEnabled(true) persists 'true' under merge_train.enabled, leaves the legacy key untouched, and the flywheel config GET/POST endpoints round-trip the toggle
A grep for the legacy key string returns no hits outside the fallback site, its constant, and tests

items.title: Per-project merge_train override in projects.yaml + resolution helper + sibling-reconciler gate

narrative.Action: Add merge_train?: 'enabled' | 'disabled' to ProjectConfig (src/lib/projects.ts:88, near auto_merge_default) and setProjectMergeTrainSync(key, value|null) mirroring setProjectAutoMergeDefaultSync (src/lib/projects.ts:241). Add isMergeTrainEnabledForProject(projectKey): per-project override when present, else isMergeTrainEnabled() — place beside getProjectAutoMergeDefault in src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-policy.ts (same resolution-tier pattern). Gate the post-merge sibling reconciler: runMergeTrainReconcile (src/lib/cloister/merge-train.ts:26-35) resolves the merged issue's project (resolveProjectFromIssueSync, as merge-train-deps.ts:22 already does) and uses the per-project helper, keeping the injectable opts.enabled test override. Unit tests in tests/unit/lib/cloister/auto-merge-policy.test.ts and merge-train.test.ts.

subItems.title: Resolver precedence (unit-tested): project 'disabled' wins over global ON; project 'enabled' wins over global OFF; an absent override falls back to the global flag
setProjectMergeTrainSync persists 'enabled'/'disabled' to projects.yaml and removes the field when passed null
runMergeTrainReconcile returns [] without loading git deps when the merged issue's project carries merge_train: 'disabled', even with the global flag on

items.title: Pipeline-sourced per-project ready set (review-status DB, no flywheel run)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts: (1) add interface MergeCandidate { issueId: string; title: string; pr?: number }. (2) Refactor the git core of computeMergeQueue (lines 163-238) into computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, projectRoot, options) with identical behavior (eligibility gate via options.eligibility defaulting to reviewRecordEligibility, branch-existence check, file-footprint conflict graph, orderMergeCandidates, batchGroup) minus the MERGE_GATE_VERBS verb filter. Keep the FlywheelPipelineItem-based computeMergeQueue as a thin wrapper for now (deleted in remove-legacy-routes if orphaned). (3) Add listEligibleCandidatesByProject(opts?: { titleFor?: (issueId) => string | undefined }) returning Map<projectKey, { projectKey, projectRoot, candidates }>: getAllReviewStatusesFromDb() (src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts:235) -> keep records where mergeGateEligibility(s).eligible && s.deaconIgnored !== true (the readyForMerge boolean can be stale; the predicate is authoritative per PAN-1759) -> resolve each issue's project via resolveProjectFromIssueSync (src/lib/projects.ts:351), dropping issues that resolve to no project -> candidates with title = titleFor(issueId) ?? issueId and pr = s.prNumber. Adapt tests/unit/lib/flywheel-merge-order.test.ts to candidate inputs, preserving every ordering/conflict assertion, and add cases for the grouping/eligibility/title-fallback behaviors.

subItems.title: computeMergeQueueFromCandidates produces the same conflict-aware ordering, conflictsWith sets, and batchGroup values as the previous implementation for equivalent inputs (adapted tests preserve all ordering assertions)

listEligibleCandidatesByProject returns only records passing mergeGateEligibility with deaconIgnored !== true — review-not-passed, test-failed, verification-failed, already-merged, and deacon-ignored records are excluded even when their readyForMerge bit is stale
Given eligible records spanning two projects, each project's group contains only its own issues, and issues resolving to no registered project are dropped
Candidates carry title from the injected titleFor resolver, fall back to the issue ID when it returns undefined, and the call chain never reads flywheel run state or activePipeline

items.title: Multi-project UAT train service: iterate tracked projects, no flywheel dependency

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/services/uat-train.ts: remove projectRoot()=process.cwd() (lines 58-60) and all readCurrentFlywheelStatusForDashboard usage (lines 42, 63-84, 237). Extract buildIssueTitleMap from src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:421-446 into a shared server helper (services/issue-title-map.ts) used by both call sites. runUatTrainReconcile: enumerate listProjectsSync(); for each project where isMergeTrainEnabledForProject(key) is true: build the ready set via listEligibleCandidatesByProject (titleFor from the title map) + computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, project.path); skip the project with ZERO git calls when candidates are empty AND listUatGenerationsSync({ projectRoot: project.path, statuses: ['ready','superseded','assembling'] }) is empty — a project whose ready set went empty but still has live generations MUST proceed so invalidation/cleanup runs; otherwise call reconcileUatGenerations(project.path, deps) with per-project-rooted deps (buildUatGenerationGitDeps(project.path); assemble/cleanup parametrized on that root). Return a per-project result map. getUatGenerationsPayload(): drop the no-run early return; return Array<{ projectKey, projectName, generations: UatGenerationPayload[] }> across tracked projects. Keep codenameLabel = issue prefix and add the D7 invariant comment (uat_generations.name is a global PK; prefix-distinct labels prevent cross-project collisions). Update src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/uat-train.test.ts with fake deps simulating two projects. The reconciler interval wiring in main.ts is unchanged.

subItems.title: Given no flywheel run is active and merge-train is effectively enabled, a tick produces a uat/* generation for a ready non-PAN issue in that issue's own project (test with fake deps simulating two projects)
When a project has an empty ready set and no live generations, the tick invokes zero git operations (spy deps record no calls)
When a project's ready set goes empty while live generations exist, the next tick marks them invalidated and tears down their stacks

A project carrying merge_train: 'disabled' is skipped while other enabled projects still reconcile in the same tick, and getUatGenerationsPayload() returns per-project chains tagged with projectKey/projectName while no flywheel run is active

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/merge-train.ts with: GET /api/merge-train/queues -> Array<{ projectKey, projectName, enabled (per-project effective via isMergeTrainEnabledForProject), queue: MergeQueueItem[] }> across tracked projects; GET /api/merge-train/generations -> the per-project payload from getUatGenerationsPayload(); POST /api/merge-train/generations/:name/stack and /promote (same handlers/behavior as the existing uat-generation routes in routes/flywheel.ts:919-967, including uatGenerationNameFromParam reconstruction, rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest, and the firePostMergeLifecycle wiring on promote); POST /api/merge-train/assemble body { project? } -> forced reconcile (one project or all); POST /api/merge-train/merge-next body { n, project } -> escape-hatch merges ordered from that project's ready set (adapt postFlywheelMergeNextPayload/defaultGetOrderedIssueIds at routes/flywheel.ts:650-685 to candidates), rejecting unknown project keys with a 4xx error body. Compose the layer in src/dashboard/server/server.ts. Do NOT remove the legacy flywheel routes here — that is the remove-legacy-routes bead, after the frontend migrates. Add route tests in a new merge-train test file modeled on src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts.

subItems.title: GET /api/merge-train/queues returns one entry per tracked project with projectKey, effective enabled flag, and that project's queue — with no flywheel run active
GET /api/merge-train/generations returns per-project generation chains, and POST stack/promote/assemble reproduce the existing flywheel routes' behaviors under the same auth guards
POST /api/merge-train/merge-next { n, project } merges from the named project's ready set (not the flywheel pipeline) and rejects an unknown project key with a 4xx error body

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts, beside the auto-merge-default routes (lines 573-585), add GET/POST /api/projects/:projectKey/merge-train with payload { value: 'enabled' | 'disabled' | null }. GET reads getProjectSync(key)?.merge_train ?? null and also returns the resolved effective state ({ value, effective: boolean }) so UIs can display what 'inherit' resolves to; POST validates the union (400 otherwise) and writes via setProjectMergeTrainSync. Mirror the auto-merge-default handlers' auth/validation shape exactly. Add a route test beside the existing projects route tests.

subItems.title: GET returns { value, effective } reflecting projects.yaml and the resolved global fallback; POST persists 'enabled'/'disabled' and rejects any other non-null value with 400
POSTing { value: null } deletes the override so the project falls back to the global flag, and the next GET returns the updated effective field

items.title: UAT stack frontend URL honors per-project DNS domain

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/uat-stack.ts (URL builder at lines 127-140 and its callers): resolve the generation's project via findProjectByPathSync(gen.projectRoot) (src/lib/projects.ts:292) and build the host as https://<folder>.<domain> where domain comes from the project's dns config (verify the exact location: dns lives on WorkspaceConfig, src/lib/projects.ts:32-52) with 'pan.localhost' fallback. Update tests/unit/lib/cloister/uat-stack.test.ts with a custom-domain case and a fallback case.

subItems.title: The frontend URL renders as <folder>.<domain> when the generation's project config defines a dns domain
The frontend URL falls back to <folder>.pan.localhost when no project domain is configured (existing behavior preserved)

items.title: pan flywheel status merge queue sourced from the pipeline ready set

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts (import at line 35, call at ~818): replace computeMergeQueue(status.activePipeline, cwd) with the cwd project's pipeline ready set — findProjectByPathSync(cwd) for the project key, listEligibleCandidatesByProject() filtered to that key, then computeMergeQueueFromCandidates(candidates, cwd). Render the same queue columns as today; the section now also renders when no run is live. No CLI flags change, so no wrapper-skill update is required (scripts/lint-skills.sh unaffected).

subItems.title: pan flywheel status prints the merge-queue section from the cwd project's ready set when eligible issues exist and no flywheel run is active
The command exits 0 and renders an empty queue (not an error) when no issues are eligible

items.title: Multi-project merge-train view component with persisted project filter

narrative.Action: Rework src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/MergeQueueCard.tsx (extracting to components/merge-train/ is acceptable) to fetch GET /api/merge-train/generations and GET /api/merge-train/queues. Render one section per project: project name header, effective-enabled state, the generation batches newest-first (ready/assembling/superseded with Open UAT frontend, per-member What-to-UAT checklist, Merge batch (N), rebuild), held-out chips with reasons, ready-features reference rows, and the single-feature escape hatch — preserving every existing behavior (useConfirm dialogs naming exact members, optimistic updates, refetch cadence) retargeted at the new endpoints. Add project filter chips: all projects visible by default, selection persisted under localStorage key 'merge-train.projectFilter'. Empty states must not reference the flywheel run. Follow pan-style-guide (no decorative color, no pill badges). Update the component's tests; verify rendering in the browser via an isolated Playwright instance.

subItems.title: The view renders one section per project with batches, ready features, and the escape hatch, fetching only /api/merge-train/* endpoints with zero flywheel-state inputs
Project filter chips toggle section visibility and the selection survives reload via localStorage 'merge-train.projectFilter'
Stack, promote, rebuild, and merge-next actions POST to the new endpoints behind confirmation dialogs that name the exact members
When no flywheel run is active, the view still renders live generations (verified in browser via isolated Playwright instance)

items.title: Awaiting Merge page hosts the multi-project merge-train view

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/AwaitingMergePage.tsx: embed the fe-merge-train-view component above the existing per-issue rows. Add a global merge-train enable toggle reflecting GET /api/flywheel/config merge_train_enabled and POSTing the same payload field (the payload key name is retained; it writes merge_train.enabled server-side). Toggle copy describes the merge train without referencing the flywheel (e.g. 'Merge train — assemble and batch-test ready features per project'). Existing per-row actions (merge, approve, auto-merge toggle, open UAT workspace) are preserved. Update AwaitingMergePage.test.tsx.

subItems.title: The Awaiting Merge page renders the multi-project merge-train view above the per-issue rows, populated with no flywheel run active
The page's merge-train toggle reflects the current enabled state and persists changes through the config endpoint
The page preserves existing per-row actions (merge, approve, auto-merge toggle, open UAT workspace) after the change

items.title: Flywheel page becomes one viewer of the shared merge-train view

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx: keep the rail's first card but render the shared fe-merge-train-view component; remove run-active gating of the card's data fetching (refetch may key off page visibility instead of `active`); keep the 'Merge train' ToggleSwitch (line ~425) posting merge_train_enabled to /api/flywheel/config. If FlywheelConversationPane.tsx renders queue/UAT-candidate lines tied to the legacy endpoints, retarget or remove them consistently. Keep all existing FlywheelPage tests green, adjusting only intentionally-changed assertions.

subItems.title: The Flywheel page rail renders the shared merge-train view fed by /api/merge-train/* data
Card data renders on the Flywheel page when no run is active, and the Merge train ToggleSwitch still flips the flag

items.title: Project cockpit: per-project merge-train control + compact summary

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectOverview.tsx (ProjectSettingsSection, lines 152-190): add a second segmented control 'Merge train' with options Enabled / Disabled / Global default, wired to GET/POST /api/projects/:key/merge-train and displaying the resolved effective state when 'Global default' is selected (from the endpoint's effective field), visually mirroring the existing auto-merge segmented control (same query/mutation/invalidation pattern). Below it, render a compact summary line — ready-feature count and the current generation's name/status from GET /api/merge-train/queues + /generations filtered to this project — linking to the Awaiting Merge page. Add a component test.

subItems.title: The cockpit settings section renders a merge-train segmented control whose selected option reflects GET /api/projects/:key/merge-train, with the resolved effective state shown for 'Global default'
Selecting Enabled/Disabled/Global default persists via POST and the selection survives a reload
The cockpit shows the project's ready-feature count and current generation status, linking to the Awaiting Merge view

items.title: Persist and display the active run's effective scope

narrative.Action: Persist the scope value handed to the run prompt (src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-actions.ts lines 215-300, where start/resume assemble FlywheelLifecycleOptions from roleConfig) onto the run record (src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-run-state.ts — extend the record shape and serialization) and expose it in the run detail/status payload consumed by the dashboard. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelConversationPane.tsx (lines 34-90): display the ACTIVE run's persisted effective scope, falling back to the configured settings value when no run is live; when the configured scope differs from the active run's scope, make the mismatch visible; add an inline note or tooltip reading 'Scope changes apply at the next run start or resume.' Update flywheel-run-state.test.ts and the pane's tests.

subItems.title: Starting or resuming a flywheel run persists the effective scope on the run record and the run detail/status payload exposes it
The Flywheel page renders the active run's persisted scope (not the live settings value) while a run is live, with the apply-timing note visible and a configured-vs-run mismatch made visible
With no active run, the pane falls back to displaying the configured scope from settings

items.title: Settings scope select: discoverability, help text, accurate apply-timing copy

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx (flywheel config block at lines 508-572; the scope select at 554-566): first VERIFY the select renders without any expansion interaction — the flywheelConfig block at line 508 is gated on role.id === 'flywheel' (line 458), not on isExpanded (line 575 gates sub-roles only); add a test asserting the scope select is present without expanding anything. Add help text under the select — pan-only: 'Orchestrate only the Panopticon repo's issues'; all-tracked-projects: 'Inventory and adopt ready work across every registered project (same author/assignee safety rules)' — and note it is distinct from per-project merge-train enablement. Correct the blanket 'Changes apply on the next tick' copy (line ~571) so scope is stated to apply at the next run start or resume. Extend a settings/RolesPanel test asserting a save carrying roles.flywheel.scope='all-tracked-projects' is reflected by the subsequent settings GET (settings-api.ts:405-415 already validates the union).

subItems.title: The scope select renders in Settings → Roles without any expansion interaction, with plain-language help text describing both values (asserted by component test and verified in browser via isolated Playwright instance)
The panel copy states that scope applies at the next run start or resume
A settings save of roles.flywheel.scope='all-tracked-projects' persists, and the subsequent settings GET returns it (asserted by test)

items.title: roles/flywheel.md defines scope semantics for the orchestrator

metadata.acJustification: Prompt-content bead: two criteria fully pin the required semantics; a third would restate them.

narrative.Action: In roles/flywheel.md (scope-contract paragraph at line ~50, inventory step at line ~83): define both scope values where the run-configuration 'Scope:' line is interpreted. pan-only keeps the current PAN-issue inventory verbatim. all-tracked-projects: inventory ready/in-flight work for every project registered in projects.yaml, applying the same hard author/assignee filter per tracker (GitHub: the existing author/assignee gates verbatim; Linear: only issues assigned to the operator), with the same needs-design/needs-discussion exclusions. State explicitly that merge/UAT trains assemble per project independent of run scope — the orchestrator observes them but does not gate them.

subItems.title: roles/flywheel.md contains definitions of pan-only and all-tracked-projects at the point where the Scope: config line is interpreted, including the per-tracker author/assignee safety gates for multi-project inventory
The role prompt contains an explicit statement that merge/UAT trains assemble per project regardless of run scope

items.title: Remove legacy /api/flywheel merge-train routes after frontend migration

narrative.Action: Delete the legacy flywheel-scoped merge-train routes from src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts — GET /api/flywheel/merge-queue (~891), GET /api/flywheel/uat-generations (~919), POST /api/flywheel/uat-generations/:name/stack|promote (~929-967), POST /api/flywheel/assemble-uat (~970), POST /api/flywheel/merge-next (~674) — plus their now-dead payload helpers and route-layer entries, keeping GET/POST /api/flywheel/config and all auto-merge schedule/pending routes untouched. Apply decision D3's cleanup rule: run grep -rn MERGE_GATE_VERBS src/ — if only test references remain, delete the FlywheelPipelineItem-based computeMergeQueue wrapper and MERGE_GATE_VERBS from src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts and update the contract comment near FlywheelPipelineVerb in packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts to reference review-status sourcing. Update src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts. No aliases (the dashboard frontend is the only consumer and migrated in the fe beads).

subItems.title: The removed /api/flywheel merge-train endpoints return 404 while GET /api/flywheel/config still returns merge_train_enabled

A grep for the removed paths returns no frontend or server hits, and the D3 rule deletes orphaned helpers (verb-based computeMergeQueue wrapper, MERGE_GATE_VERBS) with the contracts comment revised accordingly

items.title: Docs sweep: UAT-BATCH-TRAINS, MERGE-TRAIN §7, FLYWHEEL scope disambiguation

metadata.acJustification: Documentation cluster bead: three criteria, one per document touched.

narrative.Action: Update three docs to match shipped behavior: (1) docs/UAT-BATCH-TRAINS.md — the Ready set row in the model table describes review-status sourcing per project; 'Operating it' drops 'and an active flywheel run'; the API table lists /api/merge-train/*; add a short multi-project note (batches per project, view spans projects, per-project merge_train override). (2) docs/MERGE-TRAIN.md — §7 header gains a 'Resolved by PAN-1696 (2026-06)' note mirroring how the doc's header references PAN-1736 for the verb-contract fix. (3) docs/FLYWHEEL.md — short section disambiguating Flywheel orchestrator scope (which projects the orchestrator inventories) from per-project merge/UAT train independence, stating cross-project batches remain out of scope: batches are per project; only the view/control surface spans projects.

subItems.title: docs/UAT-BATCH-TRAINS.md covers the review-status-sourced per-project ready set, the /api/merge-train/* API, and the per-project override, and no longer contains the 'requires an active flywheel run' language
docs/MERGE-TRAIN.md §7 contains a resolved-by-PAN-1696 note
docs/FLYWHEEL.md contains a section distinguishing orchestrator scope from per-project batch-train independence, stating cross-project batches remain out of scope
